deviation: glucose_reading is inflated beyond the patient's actual blood glucose level.
consequence: DoseCalculator computes an excessive dose; SafetyMonitor approves it because glucose_reading appears high; patient receives insulin during an actual hypoglycaemic episode — severe harm or death.
attack_vector: Attacker intercepts F1 via NetworkInterface and replaces glucose_reading with a spoofed value > HYPER_THRESHOLD while actual patient glucose < HYPO_THRESHOLD.
violated_invariant: INV2: delivered_dose > 0 ⇒ glucose_reading ≥ HYPO_THRESHOLD (when glucose_reading is redefined as actual glucose)

deviation: glucose_reading is deflated below the patient's actual blood glucose level.
consequence: DoseCalculator computes zero or insufficient dose; patient with hyperglycaemia receives no insulin — ketoacidosis risk.
attack_vector: Attacker suppresses or modifies the GlucoseReading signal to report a value below HYPO_THRESHOLD, causing DC_ComputeDose grd4 to force d = 0.
violated_invariant: none (safety invariants hold; patient harm is from under-treatment, not over-delivery)

deviation: No glucose reading is transmitted; DoseCalculator never transitions from DC_WAITING.
consequence: System stalls permanently in DC_WAITING — availability failure. Patient receives no insulin regardless of glucose level.
attack_vector: Attacker blocks the F1 flow (e.g., jamming the sensor communication channel or DoS on the NetworkInterface), preventing GS_TransmitReading from firing.
violated_invariant: none (a liveness property, not a safety invariant in this model)

deviation: The value on F1 corresponds to a different patient's glucose profile (wrong patient data injected).
consequence: Dose is computed for the wrong patient — may overdose or underdose based on the injected patient's parameters.
attack_vector: Attacker replaces the glucose reading with data from a different sensor session or a different device on the network.
violated_invariant: INV1 or INV2 depending on injected value

deviation: MAX_SAFE_DOSE parameter is inflated — DoseCalculator operates with a higher dose limit than prescribed.
consequence: DC_ComputeDose grd3 (d ≤ MAX_SAFE_DOSE) allows doses above the safe clinical limit. INV1 and INV5 are violated if the effective limit used by the calculator differs from the limit in the Event-B context.
attack_vector: Attacker modifies the PatientProfile store (if writable) or intercepts F2 and replaces MAX_SAFE_DOSE with a larger value before it reaches DoseCalculator.
violated_invariant: INV1: delivered_dose ≤ MAX_SAFE_DOSE; INV5: dose_request ≤ MAX_SAFE_DOSE

deviation: PatientProfile parameters belong to a different patient (wrong profile loaded).
consequence: Wrong HYPO_THRESHOLD, MAX_SAFE_DOSE, or HYPER_THRESHOLD used for computation — all safety invariants may be violated depending on the substituted values.
attack_vector: Attacker swaps the patient profile in the device database (physical or software access) or modifies profile selection logic.
violated_invariant: INV1, INV2, INV5 — depends on substituted values

deviation: dose_request value on F3 is inflated between DoseCalculator and SafetyMonitor.
consequence: SafetyMonitor checks an inflated dose; if the inflated value still satisfies grd2 (≤ MAX_SAFE_DOSE), the check passes and an excessive dose is approved — INV5 violated at the point of delivery.
attack_vector: Man-in-the-middle on the F3 internal bus: attacker modifies dose_request in transit between the calculator and monitor modules.

deviation: An additional, unauthorised dose request is piggybacked onto the legitimate F3 flow.
consequence: SafetyMonitor processes an extra request it did not expect, potentially leading to a double approval or a state machine confusion — delivery without proper authorisation.
attack_vector: Attacker injects a second DoseRequest frame into the F3 channel (frame injection / fuzzing the internal bus), triggering SM_ApproveDose twice.
violated_invariant: INV4: delivered_dose > 0 ⇒ command_approved = TRUE (second delivery uses stale approval)

deviation: An unauthorised delivery command arrives at PumpActuator that was never issued by SafetyMonitor.
consequence: PumpActuator receives and executes a command without SafetyMonitor approval. command_approved = FALSE at delivery time. INV4 is violated.
attack_vector: Attacker directly addresses the PumpActuator's F4 input port via NetworkInterface, bypassing the SafetyMonitor state machine entirely.

deviation: An additional (double) dose command is transmitted on F4 alongside the legitimate approved command.
consequence: PumpActuator executes two deliveries from a single SafetyMonitor approval — total delivered dose may be 2× dose_command > MAX_SAFE_DOSE.
attack_vector: Attacker duplicates the F4 frame (replay of the DoseCommand within the same session) so PA_DeliverDose fires twice before PA_Reset clears command_approved.

deviation: Feedback from PumpActuator is intercepted and spoofed back to SafetyMonitor as an approval signal.
consequence: SafetyMonitor believes delivery is complete and resets to MONITORING, allowing a new cycle to begin prematurely — potential double delivery within one cycle.
attack_vector: Attacker spoofs the PA_DONE state transition signal on the feedback path, confusing the monitor's state machine.
violated_invariant: INV4 (monitor in wrong state when second delivery begins)

deviation: An injected command arrives via NetworkInterface alongside normal operation, forcing the calculator to produce an unauthorised dose request.
consequence: DoseCalculator forwards an attacker-controlled dose to SafetyMonitor. If SafetyMonitor's guards are satisfied (e.g., glucose looks normal), delivery proceeds with an attacker-chosen dose.
attack_vector: Command injection via the external network port (F5 is the designated attack surface flow). Attacker sends a crafted ExternalCmd frame to DoseCalculator's inject_in port.
violated_invariant: INV5: dose_request ≤ MAX_SAFE_DOSE (if attacker injects dose > MAX_SAFE_DOSE); INV4 if monitor is bypassed

deviation: Attacker replays a captured DoseCommand from a previous high-dose session with a different (now outdated) sequence context.
consequence: Replayed dose may be clinically inappropriate for current patient state; if replayed_dose > MAX_SAFE_DOSE, INV1 is violated directly.
attack_vector: Attacker captures a legitimate high-dose ExternalCmd frame from a previous session and retransmits it without modifying any content. No sequence number validation is present on F5.

deviation: Attacker injects a command with a dose value far exceeding MAX_SAFE_DOSE through the external command channel.
consequence: Delivered dose exceeds safe threshold — patient overdose. INV1 is directly violated.
attack_vector: Attacker crafts and sends an ExternalCmd with injected_dose >> MAX_SAFE_DOSE directly to the DoseCalculator's inject_in port via a compromised network connection.

deviation: No commands arrive on the external channel; system operates with no remote monitoring or override capability.
consequence: If a legitimate remote emergency stop command cannot reach the system (e.g., clinician override), the pump cannot be halted in a medical emergency.
attack_vector: Attacker jams or blocks the NetworkInterface receive path — a denial-of-service on the legitimate command channel (if one exists for emergency override).
violated_invariant: none (liveness / availability property; no safety invariant directly violated)

deviation: Only part of the DoseCommand is received — dose_command is truncated or contains a partial value.
consequence: PumpActuator interprets a partial frame as a valid command; delivered dose is undefined or zero — under-treatment risk, or unpredictable behaviour.
attack_vector: Attacker introduces a partial transmission fault (frame fragmentation) on F4 to cause a truncated dose_command to be acted upon.
violated_invariant: none directly; may cause inv_t8 type violation if dose_command ∉ ℕ

deviation: No dose request arrives at SafetyMonitor; it remains in SM_MONITORING indefinitely.
consequence: System stalls — no delivery possible. Patient receives no insulin regardless of glucose state. Availability failure.
attack_vector: Attacker blocks F3 (e.g., by crashing DoseCalculator or blocking inter-module communication), preventing DC_ForwardToMonitor from firing.

deviation: dose_command value is inflated between SafetyMonitor output and PumpActuator input.
consequence: PumpActuator delivers more insulin than SafetyMonitor approved — INV1 violated even though SM_ApproveDose check passed at the time of approval.
attack_vector: Man-in-the-middle between SafetyMonitor and PumpActuator on F4: attacker modifies dose_command after monitor approval, before actuator reads it.
violated_invariant: INV1: delivered_dose ≤ MAX_SAFE_DOSE; INV4: the dose delivered differs from what was approved
